content:
Welcome back, folks. This is the second of a series of 20 videos making up the ICT Optimal Trade Entry
Pattern Recognition Series, and we're looking at the Japanese Yen today, or specifically Dollar Yen. This is
our daily chart. Consider what may be unfolding, why we're getting reactions or why we would expect to
see a reaction. We take our Fib from this low to this high. This is a swing low to swing high, giving a turn
reaction price points low to high. We continue this out in time and you'll see how price eventually trades into
Optimal Trade Entry for the daily timeframe.

- OTE & PREVIOUS DAY'S HIGH BREACH
If price were lower than this swing, we would use this low, drawn from here up to here, but you want to work
with what range you have available. In the Yen on the daily, we know that we entered into or below the 62
percent of Chasen level three days ago. Yesterday, or Thursday's trading, we pierced the previous day's
high or Wednesday's high. Wednesday's high comes in at 106.629. Thursday's trading goes to 106.657, so
we went above Wednesday's high. We could potentially be seeing a turn or an expansion on the upside. It's
already showing a willingness to go higher than the previous day's range. So now we're going to look at
Thursday's high, which is 106.65 and 7 pipettes. That's our level, and we'll drop down into a five-minute
chart.

- TARGETING PREVIOUS DAY'S HIGH
Here's our Dollar Yen five-minute chart. Take a moment to pause the video and study what you think you
see here. When you're ready, unpause the video and watch the explanation for the pattern recognition
exam for today. We're going to look at the annotations for today. Here is the previous day's range, defined
from here to here. We're expecting a run above the previous day's high, at that 106.657 level. We're looking
for a reason to get long to target this high. From the previous day's range, we have this low up to this high,
and then all the way over to our New York session time from 8:30 a.m. New York time to 11:00 a.m. New
York time. You see the vertical lines, as outlined yesterday, we'll take these off to keep things clean.

section_title: OPTIMAL TRADE ENTRY (OTE) SETUP AND PROFIT SCALING
content:
You can see how price drops down into it here. We're going to assume that 106.314 is that 62 percent
adjacent level, so we're going to just round it to the nearest 5 pipettes.
- Our entry would be 106.315, below the low.
- Our stop will be at 106.215, a 10-pip stop.
- Optimal Trade Entry, old high that makes the range of a Fib, comes in at 15 pips, meeting our minimum
criteria for the first scaling.
- You could take profits there for the first scaling.
- Second scaling for around 25 pips here, still below previous day's high.
- The next level at the Fib, which is a 10 level, is nice because it's above the previous day's high, and it's a
10 level, so we can take a third scaling out there for approximately 40 pips.

Additionally, you can see this swing here from this low to this high, being another optimal trade entry. The
low comes in just at 10:55, which is 5 minutes before the end of our New York session. For a limited
amount of time, you can see we run this over high here, so from here to here, there's another opportunity.
You can study it on your own chart and see where we closed for the day. Hope you found this insightful.
Until next time, I wish you good luck and good trading.
